data.title: European gas jumps as Trump orders Iran blockade; supply fears deepen
data.description:
European natural gas futures leaped more than 9% to €47.8 per MMBtu on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a maritime blockade on Iranian ports after talks with the Middle Eastern country failed. 

The blockade, which is expected to begin on Monday, April 13, at 10 a.m. ET is the latest escalation in the U.S.-Iran conflict. Global energy supply will be further impeded due to this move.

The decision will apply to vessels of all nations traveling to or from Iranian ports and coastal areas, including those along the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. CENTCOM said the measures will be implemented impartially.

European natural gas rebounded on Monday from an over five-week low. The United States Natural Gas Fund (UNG) was up 1.7% to $10.95 at the time of writing, rebounding after three consecutive sessions of losses. 

Middle Eastern gas exports typically go to Asia; however, if the disruption continues for a long time, Europe could be facing a shortage ahead of the next winter. 

The LNG supply crunch has been ongoing for over a month, especially as liquefied natural gas shipments from the Gulf dried up following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in March. At the same time, Qatar, which supplies 20% of the world's LNG, halted exports after missile strikes damaged its key facility. 

Nations across Asia and Europe are now competing for alternative supplies from the U.S. and other producers at sharply higher prices. In response, some governments have begun rationing energy or reducing workweeks to conserve fuel.

data.title: Trump labor board pushes to settle major Amazon contractor case: report
data.description:
The federal government is moving to settle a yearslong case over Amazon.com Inc.’s (AMZN) treatment of a group of delivery drivers, averting what could have been a landmark ruling establishing the company as the boss of some of the workers it has long insisted aren’t its employees, Bloomberg reported.

Since 2024, the National Labor Relations Board has been prosecuting Amazon for allegedly violating the rights of a group of drivers employed by one of the company’s former “delivery service partners,” a contractor in Palmdale, Calif. called Battle-Tested Strategies.

As part of the case, the NLRB’s general counsel has argued that Amazon (AMZN) was the drivers’ “joint employer,” meaning it had enough control over them to be liable for their treatment and was obligated to collectively bargain when they unionized with the Teamsters. This meant, the government alleged, Amazon’s refusal to negotiate with the union was illegal, too, the report said.

Amazon denied wrongdoing and said it was refusing to comply with the government’s subpoenas about whether it was a joint employer, which it described as a “fishing expedition” that the labor board was inappropriately pursuing because of ulterior motives.

Testimony in the ongoing trial had been slated to resume April 13. But on Sunday, an attorney for the NLRB’s general counsel sent the judge considering the case a proposed settlement to end it instead. Under the proposed deal, a copy of which was viewed by Bloomberg Businessweek, the company would provide two weeks’ pay to as many as 84 workers who had been employed by BTS, without admitting wrongdoing or being found liable as a joint employer.

If approved, the settlement would defuse one of the agency’s most consequential cases against Amazon, one that could have led for the first time to an agency judge, the NLRB members in Washington, and, eventually, federal appeals court judges ruling that Amazon was the joint employer of drivers for one of its delivery service partners, the report added.

To serve as NLRB general counsel, President Trump appointed Crystal Carey, a partner at the law firm Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP, whose clients include Amazon.

data.title: Beyond Meat adds new breakfast sausage products to its lineup
data.description:
Beyond Meat (BYND) announced the nationwide rollout of its latest Beyond Breakfast Sausage lineup at major retailers. The product rollout will include placement at Kroger (KR) and Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM), with Whole Foods Market locations coming soon.

The Beyond Breakfast Sausage lineup was said to include clean, simple ingredients, including heart-healthy avocado oil. The lineup features  7–9 grams of plant protein per serving, just 0.5 grams of saturated fat per serving, and no cholesterol, GMOs, or added hormones or antibiotics. It is also the first plant-based breakfast sausage to earn Clean Label Project Certification.

The company highlighted that as  protein continues to dominate consumer nutrition priorities, with 70% of Americans actively trying to increase intake and nearly half prioritizing protein at breakfast, the brand is leaning into the morning occasion with great-tasting and convenient plant protein options made from clean ingredients designed to help consumers fuel their day.

"We’re thrilled to bring our new Beyond Breakfast Sausage lineup to retailers nationwide, giving consumers more opportunities to fuel their bodies with these Clean Label Project Certified products," stated Beyond Meat (BYND) CEO  Ethan Brown. "By combining great taste with thoughtfully chosen ingredients, we’re making it easier than ever to start your day with Beyond," he added.

data.title: Somnigroup to buy Leggett & Platt in ~$2.5B all-stock deal
data.description:
Somnigroup International (SGI) has agreed to acquire Leggett & Platt (LEG) in an all-stock transaction worth ~$2.5B, based on Somnigroup's April 10 closing price.

Under the terms of the agreement, Leggett & Platt shareholders will receive 0.1455 shares of Somnigroup common stock in exchange for each share of Leggett & Platt common stock they own.

As a result, Leggett & Platt's shareholders will own ~9% of the combined company on a fully diluted basis. The agreement has been unanimously approved by the boards of directors of both companies.

The transaction is currently anticipated to close by year-end 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions, including approval by Leggett & Platt's shareholders and receipt of applicable regulatory approvals.

Upon closing, Leggett & Platt is expected to operate as a separate business unit within Somnigroup, similar to Tempur Sealy, Mattress Firm, and Dreams, and to maintain its offices in Carthage, Missouri.

Leggett & Platt's chairman and CEO, Karl Glassman, will continue to lead Leggett & Platt following the closing date and will assist with a seamless transition to a new CEO of the Leggett & Platt business unit within twelve months of the closing date.

Somnigroup and Leggett & Platt have collaborated for nearly 50 years to drive innovation in the bedding market. After giving effect to the transaction, including elimination of intercompany sales, the combined company generated 2025 net sales of around $11.2B, ~$1.7B of adjusted EBITDA, and $1.1B of operating cash flow.

The combination is expected to be accretive to adjusted EPS before synergies in the first year post close and presents cost synergy opportunities with an expected net positive impact on adjusted EBITDA of $50M on a fully implemented annual run-rate basis.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Vertiv buys BMarko Structures
data.description:
Vertiv (VRT) has acquired BMarko Structures, a U.S.-based provider of custom-engineered structural fabrication.

Founded in 2014, BMarko specializes in custom-engineered builds, including steel and wood frames that align well with AI factory and data center requirements.

BMarko recently expanded its engineering and fabrication facility to approximately 560,000 square feet. This facility is located in proximity to Vertiv's Infrastructure Solutions manufacturing operations in the region.

"AI is reshaping infrastructure requirements, with customers placing greater demands on time-to-capacity, flexibility, and efficiency across the infrastructure layer," said Gio Albertazzi, CEO of Vertiv. This acquisition strengthens Vertiv's ability to help customers move faster, with better systems-level performance and control, as infrastructure demands continue to grow in complexity."

VRT -0.73% premarket to $292.94

Source: Press Release

data.title: Kalshi saw record sports trading volume during the Masters golf tournament
data.description:
Prediction market platform Kalshi (KALSHI) shattered its record for sports event volume during the 2026 Masters Tournament by reportedly generating more than $545M. Notably, the Masters was the most-bet sporting event in the company’s history.  

The 2026 Masters, won by Northern Irish golfer Rory McIlroy, eclipsed Kalshi’s previous sporting event benchmarks, including Super Bowl LIX, which generated between $871M and $1B in early 2026 but was spread across a broader array of prop bets and longer trading windows. March Madness, by comparison, drew roughly $500M combined across men’s and women’s tournaments.

The tournament’s headline market, “Masters Champion,” alone accounted for approximately $460M in trades, making it the second most-bet single market in Kalshi's (KALSHI) history. Only the “Who will win the Presidential Election?” contract, which drew $535M during the 2024 cycle, has ever surpassed that figure. Industry watchers said the dominance of the outright winner market highlighted golf's unique appeal to prediction market traders, who favor multi-day events with evolving odds and deep liquidity.

The surge around the Masters propelled Kalshi (KALSHI) to a record-breaking $3.4B of total platform volume for the week. 

The trading volume on Kalshi (KALSHI) may have held back sports betting volume for DraftKings (DKNG), FanDuel (FLUT), and BetMGM (MGM) (GMVHF) in some states and also helped to entrench the brand in states such as California and Texas, where sports betting is not legal.

data.title: Top and lowest-rated large-cap stocks heading into earnings
data.description:
With the first quarter earnings season around the corner, Seeking Alpha has curated a list of the highest- and lowest-rated large-cap stocks as per its Quant model.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

Earnings season kicks off with Goldman Sachs (GS) on Monday, followed by a wave of financial heavyweights on Tuesday, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), Citigroup (C), and BlackRock (BLK) – all rated Hold. The Financials (XLF) sector is predicted to report the third-highest year-over-year earnings growth rate of all eleven sectors for Q1 at 15.1%, according to FactSet.

Later in the week, results from Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), Netflix (NFLX), and PepsiCo (PEP) will offer insight into global demand, tech spending, and consumer trends.

Here’s a look at the top-rated large-cap stocks (market cap above $10B) as per Seeking Alpha’s quant model:

  • Sandisk (SNDK), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy
  • Lumentum Holdings (LITE), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy
  • Micron Technology (MU), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy
  • Petróleo Brasileiro S.A. - Petrobras (PBR.A), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy
  • Petróleo Brasileiro - Petrobras (PBR), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy
  • TTM Technologies (TTMI), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy
  • TotalEnergies (TTE), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM), Quant Rating 4.96 – Strong Buy
  • Eni (E), Quant Rating 4.96 – Strong Buy
  • Lynas Rare Earths (LYSDY), Quant Rating 4.96 – Strong Buy

Lowest-rated large-cap stocks:

  • Crown Castle (CCI), Quant Rating 1.10 – Strong Sell
  • Dassault Systèmes (DASTY), Quant Rating 1.19 – Strong Sell
  • Adyen (ADYEY), Quant Rating 1.23 – Strong Sell
  • Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), Quant Rating 1.25 – Strong Sell
  • Atlassian (TEAM), Quant Rating 1.26 – Strong Sell
  • Gartner (IT), Quant Rating 1.31 – Strong Sell
  • Beiersdorf Aktiengesellschaft (BDRFY), Quant Rating 1.37 – Strong Sell
  • Kuaishou Technology (KUASF), Quant Rating 1.38 – Strong Sell
  • Erie Indemnity Company (ERIE), Quant Rating 1.39 – Strong Sell
  • CSL Limited (CSLLY), Quant Rating 1.40 – Strong Sell

S&P 500 Sector Outlook

According to FactSet, eight of the eleven S&P 500 sectors are expected to report year-over-year earnings growth, led by the Information Technology, Materials, and Financials sectors. On the other hand, three sectors are predicted to report a year-over-year decline in earnings, led by the Health Care sector.

Overall, the current earnings growth rate for the S&P 500 for the first quarter is 12.6%, which would mark the sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit (year-over-year) earnings growth reported by the index.

Looking at ratings, Energy (XLE), Technology (XLK), Industrials (XLI), and Materials (XLB) were the highest-rated (“Buy”) among the 11 S&P 500 (SP500) sectors by Seeking Alpha's Quant model. Consumer Staples (XLP) and Healthcare (XLV) received Sell ratings.

Here is a list of ETFs to track the broader U.S. market: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU)

data.title: AM Markets Need to Know: Iran blockade, tariff threat on China, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures traded cautiously on Monday as investors braced for the start of a U.S.-led maritime blockade on Iranian ports while monitoring escalating trade tensions between Washington and Beijing.

Now, here are five news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

U.S. to enforce Iran blockade starting Monday: U.S. Central Command announced Sunday that it will begin enforcing a maritime blockade on all traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports starting Monday, April 13, at 10 a.m. ET, following a presidential directive. The blockade will apply to vessels of all nations traveling to or from Iranian ports and coastal areas, including those along the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. CENTCOM said the measures will be implemented impartially.

Blockade could wipe out Iran’s economy: A U.S.-led blockade on Iranian ports could effectively shut down the country’s economic model by cutting off its primary source of external income, according to economist Robin Brooks. Brooks noted that Iran’s large current account surplus, driven overwhelmingly by oil and gas exports, would be wiped out under a full maritime blockade. The bulk of Iran’s inflows comes from energy exports, which he argued would fall to zero if shipping were halted.

Saudi Arabia restores critical pipeline capacity: Saudi Arabia returned its East-West pipeline to full operating capacity of about 7 million barrels a day, reestablishing a critical route for crude shipments to the Red Sea. The pipeline had been partially disrupted after a recent strike damaged one of its 11 pumping stations along the 746-mile network, cutting flows by roughly 700,000 barrels a day. The incident came shortly after a ceasefire was announced in the Iran conflict.

Chinese rare earth producers hike prices sharply: Chinese rare earth producers’ shares moved higher on Monday after key producers announced a sharp price hike in rare earth products for Q2, underscoring tightening supply and Beijing’s continued grip on the strategic metals. China Northern Rare Earth and Baotou Steel raised the related-party transaction price for rare-earth concentrate to 38,804 yuan per metric ton for Q2 2026, up roughly 45% from 26,834 yuan in Q1.

Trump threatens 50% tariffs on China over Iran: President Donald Trump escalated geopolitical tensions on Sunday, warning Beijing that the U.S. would impose “staggering” 50% tariffs on Chinese goods if Beijing provides military aid to Iran during the current U.S.-Israeli conflict. Speaking with Maria Bartiromo on Fox News, Trump characterized the potential penalties as a direct consequence of any support for Tehran.

data.title: Robinhood said to exclude some prediction markets amid manipulation concerns
data.description:
Robinhood (HOOD) has excluded some prediction markets, the Financial Times reported, amid concerns over possible insider trading and manipulation fueled by certain well-timed bets on Polymarket (POLYMARKET) and Kalshi (KALSHI). 

"We don't necessarily offer all prediction markets or all event contracts," Jordan Sinclair, president of Robinhood UK, told FT. He added that the company was "very focused on market abuse, insider trading."

He said so-called mention markets are a type of event contract that Robinhood (HOOD) did not offer "for exactly some of those concerns."

Mention markets are for placing bets on the words that may be used in certain speeches or events, like a White House press briefing or a company's earnings call.

In February, a former editor at MrBeast – the most subscribed YouTube channel – was fined $20,000 and reported by Kalshi (KALSHI) to regulators for insider trading. Kalshi offers contracts to place wagers on what MrBeast could say in future videos.

Robinhood (HOOD) partnered with Kalshi (KALSHI) and ForecastEx to offer the prediction markets on its platform.

Prediction markets were Robinhood's (HOOD) "fastest-growing business ever" last year, according to CEO Vlad Tenev, with over 12B contracts traded on the platform.
